the_real_candleford_green.heading: The Real Candleford Green
the_real_candleford_green.about: A conversation with Martin Greenwood, author of The Real Candleford Green. He describes many of the residents of Fringford (Candleford Green) who would have provided inspiration for Flora's characters including Kezia Whitton (Dorcas Lane), the postmistress who gave Flora her first job as an assisant and letter-carrier.

on_the_trail.heading: On The Trail of Flora
on_the_trail.about: A conversation with Jo Smith (author of On The Trail of Flora Thompson) about Flora's life after leaving Fringford (Candleford Green) and the people and places that inspired her life of writing.

bmmt_01.heading: Citizen Soldiers
bmmt_01.about: A conversation with Ian Beckett from the Bucks Military Museum about the raising of local troops.

bmmt_02.heading: Shilkos, Miniatures and Pistols
bmmt_02.about: A closer look at the exhibits in the first cabinet.

bmmt_03.heading: Dead Man's Penny
bmmt_03.about: A closer look at the dead man's penny and other exibits in the second cabinet.

bmmt_04.heading: Uniforms Through Time
bmmt_04.about: Let's find out more about this hand-made set of miniature figures depicting Bucks uniforms through the ages.

bmmt_05.heading: A drum and uniform
bmmt_05.about: What the drum and uniform tell us about the remarkable achievements of the Bucks units in the First and Second World Wars
